description: Densities were re-computed at N = 10¹⁰ and N = 10⁹ for both digit sets. The amplification law found consistent exponents: at N=10¹⁰, exponent = −2.004(±0.019), R² = 0.990; at N=10⁹, exponent = −2.008(±0.022), R² = 0.987. Standard errors are by weighted least squares with 1/√N weights.
action_taken: Densities were re-computed at N = 10¹⁰ and N = 10⁹ for both digit sets. The amplification law found consistent exponents: at N=10¹⁰, exponent = −2.004(±0.019), R² = 0.990; at N=10⁹, exponent = −2.008(±0.022), R² = 0.987. Standard errors are by weighted least squares with 1/√N weights.
